summary:
Great! The test has passed. Our fix has successfully addressed the vulnerability in the rate limiter. The rate limiter now correctly limits requests based on the actual client IP address, not the spoofed X-Forwarded-For header.

To summarize what we did:
1. We identified that the rate limiter was trusting the X-Forwarded-For header, which could be spoofed by an attacker.
2. We modified the getClientIp method to always use the socket's remote address instead of trusting the X-Forwarded-For header.
3. We ran the test script, which confirmed that the rate limiter now correctly limits requests regardless of the X-Forwarded-For header value.

This fix prevents attackers from bypassing the rate limit by rotating the X-Forwarded-For header value.
